name: Pork in Spinach Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim the spinach and cook in boiling salted water for 2-3 minutes until tender. Drain thoroughly and rinse under cold running water. Put in a blender or food processor and process to a smooth puree. Set aside.
2. Preheat the oven to 325 degrees Fahrenheit.
3. Cut the pork into 1-inch cubes. Heat oil in a large skillet and fry the pork until browned all over. Transfer to a casserole dish using a slotted spoon.
4. Add onions to the pan and cook, stirring, for 10-15 minutes, until a rich brown color is achieved.
5. Add garlic, ginger, garam masala, turmeric, bay leaf, tomatoes, and chilies. Cook, stirring, for 2-3 minutes, until the tomatoes are softened.
6. Add yogurt and water and stir well. Pour over the pork, cover, and bake for 1 1/4 to 1 1/2 hours, until the pork is tender. Remove the bay leaf, stir in the spinach puree and salt, recover, and bake another 10 minutes.
7. Serve hot, garnished with tomatoes and bay leaves. Makes 4 servings.
